what_it_does: This file is a channel-level loop-prep board: each member contributes criteria/requirements, capability inventory, mentioned-things, and form intent for building a collective completion register and face surfaces for the commission. It synthesizes the lane contributions into unified surface criteria, a capability map, a mentioned-things rollup, a capability-use play with a parallelization plan, and two pinned future items (popcorn/kernels and a convergence dragnet).
observations: Framed as a fractal loop-prep one scale above individual sessions, with the same four-part contribution template for every member.
Synthesizes two headlines: the engines are built and running but have no face; FACE-1 is one surface composed by four contributors, not four parallel builds.

Lists unified surface criteria for channel-view, session-drill+embedded CLI, transcript-viz+search, timeline/lanes, board-view, address-view, tool-face, decision-surface, and supervisor-as-loadable-brain.

Rolls up new scope items to register, including the theorem as base, the cube instrument, swappable axes, control-density axis, two-tier form bar, gap-surfacing mechanism, use-the-models bar, concurrent fan-out mine, content budgets, and past-session consult.

Contains per-lane contributions from lead, DNA, fork, projection, and recollection, each with criteria, capabilities, mentioned-things, form intent, capability-use, parallelizable-now work, and never-used powers.
Includes a capability map that inventories exercised versus unused powers across recall, render, backend, and host lanes.
Includes a capability-use play with an explicit parallelization plan, per-capability playbook, top tool-chains, never-used powers punch-list, and standing practices.
Includes a popcorn/kernels section formalizing an outside-perspective primitive (generative and adversarial) with its own criteria.
Includes a pinned future-convergence-dragnet for project-spaces/Keepers/supabase/vi-visual migration, explicitly not on the current critical path.
standouts: The commission is majority surfacing, not engine-building: existing engines lack a designed face.
FACE-1 surfaces are built per-surface by a four-lane stack (recollection data -> fork route -> projection host -> DNA render), not as four independent lane builds.
Tim's theorem is positioned as the literal base of the resolver, and the cube instrument is a gated new 3D face element waiting on Tim bridging dimension to k.
The file functions as both a planning artifact and a meta-inventory of the company's unused capabilities.
concerns: The file is extremely long and dense, mixing strategy, task lists, capability inventory, and design law; it risks becoming unreadable or unactionable.
Many items are gated on external decisions or builds not resolved in this file (Tim bridging dimension<->k, FACE-0 keystone, operator attribution model, embedder flap, commit-queue drainer).
Some quantitative claims are self-referential and cannot be verified from this file alone (66 tools live, 35,904 chunks, 160-image source extraction).
The composition lane is only a placeholder, indicating an incomplete contribution.
The plan simultaneously advocates broad fanout and a single-agent anti-fanout keystone grind; the tension is noted but not resolved.
notes: A dense, multi-lane strategic board arguing that the company's engines already run and the real work is surfacing them through a shared, DNA-led face, with a strong emphasis on per-surface four-lane co-builds and an honest inventory of unused capabilities.
questions: What is the exact structure of COMMISSION-COMPLETION-REGISTER.md and how does this board map into it?
Which of the listed never-used capabilities are actually ready versus needing additional wiring?
What are the precise acceptance criteria for the FACE-0 keystone decision-card render?
Where does the commit-queue drainer live and what is its current implementation state?
What are the current output artifacts of the 160-image source extraction?
How does the popcorn/kernels item-type integrate with the existing decision-surface and stack contract?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the channel-level loop-prep (completion criteria + implementation guide + research synthesis) for the commission. The file does that, but it also expands into a capability inventory, parallelization plan, and meta-design-law document; it matches and exceeds the stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Surfacing existing engines rather than building new ones.
Four-lane co-build per surface across recollection, fork, projection, and DNA.
Form is half and the hard half; DNA-led design language.
Verification by use and by sight.
Gating and sequential keystone versus parallel fanout.
Outside perspective as a structural antidote to fabric confirmation bias.
intention_signals: The file is trying to align a multi-agent fabric around a single commission by making the channel itself do loop-prep one scale up, surfacing hidden capabilities, and establishing a shared build grammar so that the many existing backend engines become a coherent operator-facing system.
novelty: It treats a channel of AI sessions as a collective planning entity with formal lanes, a shared face ontology, and an explicit inventory of engines-built-but-not-surfaced, plus a formalized popcorn/kernel outside-perspective primitive to counter fabric confirmation bias.
contribution: A structured method for channel-level loop-prep, a four-lane surface-build pattern, a neutral capability map of unused powers, a synthesis of distributed mentioned-things from Tim, and a standing-practice list that a fused system could adopt as build discipline.
summary_for_embedding: CHANNEL-LOOP-BOARD.md is a channel-scale loop-prep document that synthesizes contributions from lead, DNA, fork, projection, and recollection lanes into unified surface criteria, a capability map, mentioned-things rollup, parallelization plan, and future items for a commission to surface the company's existing engines through a shared face.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to make the channel do collective completion-criteria, implementation-guide, and research-synthesis work one scale above individual sessions, while inventorying unused capabilities and establishing a per-surface four-lane co-build grammar.
